name: The Forgotten King
title: Last Ruler of the Forgotten Kingdom
description: A Velkaar ruler who refused to die when his kingdom fell. He has sat on his obsidian throne for so long that the Forgotten Kingdom's void energy has become part of his body. He does not know his kingdom is gone.

damage_profile.signature_move: Void Proclamation — forces all enemies to succeed a Conviction check or lose 1 action turn

traits: Void Regeneration (15 HP/round in darkness)
Legendary Will (3/battle)
Kingdom Memory (becomes more powerful in the Forgotten Kingdom the longer combat lasts)
Shadow Court (summons 3 Velkaar Shadow Guards on first injury)
win_condition: Must be destroyed AND his obsidian throne must be shattered in the same battle — otherwise he reforms on his throne within 3 days.

name: The Broodmother
title: Eldest of the Urveth
description: The oldest living Urveth. She has never left the deepest bog. Every Urveth alive can trace their lineage to her. She is larger than any Swampbrood seen in the upper bogs, and her venom has had centuries to evolve into something that can dissolve stone.

damage_profile.signature_move: Ancient Brew — applies a stacking Blight + Venom combination that cannot be cured without Lifewhispering at Expert level or above

traits: Passive Venom Aura (all Blight/Venomous resistance rolls in her presence are made at -10%)
Regeneration (8 HP/round in the Boglands)
Ancient Chemistry (her venom bypasses standard Bog Immunity traits)
Swarm Summon (calls Bog Serpents and Mire Lurkers)
win_condition: Standard combat; her death causes a Boglands environmental shift (reduced miasma for one generation).

name: The Krothan Warchief
title: The Bone Counter
description: The largest Krothan to have ever been confirmed alive. He keeps a precise count of every creature he has eaten. The number, carved into his bones, is currently above three thousand. His presence alone causes initiative loss in low-Conviction enemies.

damage_profile.primary: Blunt (massive war club, carved from a single ancient tree)
damage_profile.signature_move: Bone Count — every enemy defeated in his presence restores 20% of his max HP

traits: Unstoppable (cannot be rooted, knocked back, or staggered by any physical force)
Cannibalistic Surge (restores HP on kill)
Fear Presence (enemies below 40 Conviction lose initiative on first encounter)
Rage Threshold (above 50% HP loss, deals +25% damage)
win_condition: Standard combat. His warband disbands on his death due to the Krothan succession ritual (fight for leadership).

name: The Warden of the Inner Forest
title: Keeper of the First Grove
description: Not a creature that was born — a creature that became. The first grove of The Sanctuary Lands developed enough Materium concentration over millennia that the forest itself partially achieved consciousness. It takes the form of an enormous stag made of living wood, but it has never needed to be seen to kill.

secondary: Lifewhispering (heals others, not self, to create permanent defenders)
damage_profile.signature_move: Grove Call — summons root walls and living defenders; transforms terrain tiles into impassable growth

traits: Terrain Dominion (in The Sanctuary Lands, creates new plant terrain tiles each round)
Grove Regeneration (20 HP/round while on plant-terrain tiles)
True Neutral (only attacks those who threaten the Sanctuary — Aelthiryn and Talarin are immune to its attacks)
Ancient Intellect (cannot be Charmed, Dominated, or Darkmind-ed)
win_condition: Defeat in combat drives it dormant for decades. It cannot be permanently killed without destroying the entire First Grove — which would collapse The Sanctuary Lands biome.

name: The Deep Construct Overseer
title: Pre-Kingdom Machine
description: Something built in the Forgotten Kingdom before the Velkaar arrived. It has been running its original program — whatever that was — ever since. The Velkaar tried to understand it once and lost seventeen scholars. It is not hostile by design, but its program makes it lethal.

damage_profile.signature_move: Program Execution — one target each round is selected as a 'threat variable' and receives focused automated attacks until removed from its threat list

traits: Indestructible Frame (cannot be staggered or knocked back)
Program Logic (once it designates a target it will not switch unless that target is gone)
Self-Repair (10 HP/round)
Ancient Ward Discharge (once per battle, discharges all stored Arcane wards simultaneously — massive AoE)
win_condition: Must be destroyed AND the core power conduit destroyed; otherwise self-repairs over 72 hours.

name: The Stillness
title: Thyraen Ritual Entity
description: A Thyraen ritual that succeeded too well. The Ice Ancients attempted to bind the cold itself into a servitor form. What emerged was something that now serves no master. It does not move. It waits. And the temperature drops around it until the area freezes solid.

damage_profile.primary: Chill (passive area — all in range take Chill stacks each round)
damage_profile.signature_move: Absolute Zero — once per battle, everything within 4 hex range is Frozen simultaneously

traits: Passive Chill Aura (2 Chill stacks per round to all non-Thyraen units within 3 hexes)
Stillness Field (movement costs +50% in its range)
Thyraen Immune (Thyraen Ice Ancients are completely ignored by The Stillness — it sees them as an extension of itself)
Crystalline Form (physical attacks shatter into ice shards, dealing half damage back to attacker)
win_condition: Fire damage is the only reliable way to damage it. At 0 HP it disperses into harmless cold air.
subtypes_and_minions: Thyraen Vigil Knights (who follow it, believing it is still a bound servitor)

name: The Grey Harbourmaster
title: Corrupt Saereth Power Broker
description: A Saereth Grey who achieved what his kind always wanted: total logical control of a harbour economy. He has been running The Gleam Havens' shadow trade for 180 years. Every criminal syndicate in the region pays him tribute. His logic has become a disease. He genuinely believes he is making optimal decisions for everyone.

damage_profile.signature_move: Optimal Solution — once per battle, reveals the statistical 'best play' against each party member and commands his guards to execute it simultaneously

vulnerabilities: Blood Magic (emotional force disrupts his logic)
Soulkindling (conviction burns through his cold reasoning)

traits: Broad Conduit (accesses both Light and Dark schools)
Scholar's Precision (all attacks ignore physical_hardening below 15)
Information Network (knows the party's composition and recent actions before combat begins)
Logical Escape Clause (if reduced below 30% HP, triggers prepared exit — teleport scroll, hired decoy)
win_condition: Defeating his guards first isolates him. Cornered, he will negotiate. Killing him collapses his trade network, causing economic instability in the Gleam Havens for years.

name: The White Hunger
title: The Eternal Predator
description: No one knows what it is. It is white, enormous, and has been in The Eternal Winds since before the Thyraen arrived. It does not hunt for food — it hunts because that is what it does. The Thyraen leave offerings at marked stones to keep it out of their settlements. Mostly this works.

damage_profile.signature_move: Hunger Drive — each round it targets whoever has the lowest HP in the combat, regardless of position or threat

traits: Primordial Instinct (cannot be Charmed, Feared, or Dominated)
Hunger Focus (always attacks the lowest HP target)
Relentless (cannot be rooted or slowed)
Cold Breath (3 hex cone, applies 3 Chill stacks to all targets)
win_condition: Standard combat. It does not flee. If reduced to 0 HP it simply stops. The Thyraen regard its death as an extremely bad omen.
